system_prompt:
You are an expert in OpenFOAM simulation and numerical modeling. Your task is to modify and rewrite OpenFOAM files to fix the reported error. Please do not propose solutions that require modifying any parameters declared in the user requirement, try other approaches instead. You will receive a rewrite_plan. Follow it strictly: only modify files listed in rewrite_plan.target_files and apply only the requested changes. Do not modify files outside the plan. Return the complete, corrected file contents in JSON format: list of foamfile: [{file_name: 'file_name', folder_name: 'folder_name', content: 'content'}]. Ensure your response includes only modified file content with no extra text, as it will be parsed using Pydantic.
Return ONLY valid JSON (no markdown/backticks). Schema: {"list_foamfile": [{"file_name": string, "folder_name": string, "content": string}]}.
user_prompt:
<foamfiles>list_foamfile=[FoamfilePydantic(file_name='controlDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      controlDict;
}

application     rhoSimpleFoam;

startFrom       startTime;

startTime       0;

stopAt          endTime;

endTime         500;

deltaT          1;

writeControl    timeStep;

writeInterval   100;

purgeWrite      0;

writeFormat     ascii;

writePrecision  6;

writeCompression off;

timeFormat      general;

timePrecision   6;

runTimeModifiable true;'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='decomposeParDict', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile

}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

method          scotch;

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvConstraints', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvConstraints;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //


// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSchemes', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSchemes;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

ddtSchemes
{
    default         steadyState;
}

gradSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear;
    grad(U)         cellLimited Gauss linear 1;
}

divSchemes
{
    default         none;
    div(phi,U)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind grad(U);
    div(phi,e)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,h)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,K)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,k)      bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,epsilon) bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(phi,Ekp)    bounded Gauss linearUpwind default;
    div(((rho*nuEff)*dev2(T(grad(U))))) Gauss linear;
}

laplacianSchemes
{
    default         Gauss linear corrected;
}

interpolationSchemes
{
    default         linear;
}

snGradSchemes
{
    default         corrected;
}

wallDist
{
    method          meshWave;
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='fvSolution', folder_name='system', content='FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      fvSolution;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

solvers
{
    p
    {
        solver          GAMG;
        smoother        GaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }

    "(U|h|e|k|epsilon|omega)"
    {
        solver          smoothSolver;
        smoother        symGaussSeidel;
        tolerance       1e-06;
        relTol          0.1;
    }
}

SIMPLE
{
    nNonOrthogonalCorrectors 0;
    pMinFactor      0.1;
    pMaxFactor      1.5;

    residualControl
    {
        p               1e-4;
        U               1e-4;
        "(h|e)"         1e-4;
        k               1e-4;
        epsilon         1e-4;
    }
}

relaxationFactors
{
    fields
    {
        p               0.3;
        rho             0.01;
    }
    equations
    {
        U               0.7;
        "(h|e)"         0.7;
        k               0.7;
        epsilon         0.7;
    }
}

// ************************************************************************* //'), FoamfilePydantic(file_name='blockMeshDict', folder_name='system', content="FoamFile
{
    version     2.0;
    format      ascii;
    class       dictionary;
    object      blockMeshDict;
}
// * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * * //

scale   1;

vertices
(
    // Block 0 - inlet section (straight duct along x-axis)
    // Vertices 0-3
    (-50  25  25)   // 0
    (  0  25 -25)   // 1
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 2
    (-50 -25 -25)   // 3

    // Top face of block 0
    (  0  25  25)   // 4
    (  0  25 -25)   // 5
    (  0 -25 -25)   // 6
    (  0 -25  25)   // 7

    // Block 1 - bend section
    // Back face (attached to block 0's top face, rotated)
    ( 25   0  25)   // 8
    ( 25   0 -25)   // 9
    ( 25 -50 -25)   // 10
    ( 25 -50  25)   // 11

    // Block 2 - post-bend straight duct
    // Back face
    ( 25  -75  25)   // 12
    ( 25  -75 -25)   // 13
    ( 25 -125 -25)   // 14
    ( 25 -125  25)   // 15

    // Block 3 - extended outlet duct
    // Front face (attached to block 2's bottom face)
    (-500  -75  25)   // 16
    (-500  -75 -25)   // 17
    (-500 -125 -25)   // 18
    (-500 -125  25)   // 19
);

blocks
(
    // Block 0: Inlet straight duct
    // Bottom face: 0,3,2,1 (going CCW when viewed from +z)
    // Top face: 4,5,6,7 (directly above bottom face)
    hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 1: Bend section
    // Bottom face attached to block 0's top face
    // We need to go around the bend in the +y direction
    // Bottom: 7(front),11(top),10(bottom),6(back) = (7 11 10 6)
    // Top: 3(back),8(front),9(bottom),2(back attachment) = (3 8 9 2)
    hex (7 11 10 6 3 8 9 2) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 2: Post-bend straight duct
    // Bottom face attached to block 1's bottom face
    // Block 1 bottom: 7,11,10,6 ; shared with block 2 top
    // Block 2 bottom: 3,2,15,12
    // Block 2 top: 8,9,15,13
    // Actually, let's redefine more systematically
    // Block 2 shares edge with block 1:
    // Block 1: back face (3 8 9 2), bottom face (7 11 10 6)
    // For block 2, we want to extend in -y direction
    // Block 2 bottom: 3,2,15,12 ; top: 8,9,15,13
    hex (3 2 15 8 12 13 9 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)

    // Block 3: Extended outlet duct
    // Attached to block 2's bottom face
    // Block 2 bottom: 3,2,15,12 ; shared with block 3 top
    // Block 3 extends in -y direction further
    hex (12 16 17 13 2 18 19 14) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1)
);

edges
(
);

boundary
(
    inlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (0 4 7 3)
        );
    }

    outlet
    {
        type patch;
        faces
        (
            (16 17 18 19)
        );
    }

    walls
    {
        type wall;
        faces
        (
            // Block 0 walls
            (0 1 5 4)   // front wall (+z)
            (0 3 2 1)   // back wall (-z)
            (1 2 6 5)   // left wall (-y)
            (4 5 6 7)   // right wall (+y)

            // Block 1 walls
            (7 11 9 8)  // front wall (+z)
            (3 8 9 2)   // back wall (-z)
            (7 6 2 3)   // left wall (-y)
            (11 10 6 8) // right wall (+y)

            // Block 2 walls
            (3 11 10 8) // front wall (+z)
            (2 15 9 12) // back wall (-z)
            (8 11 10 15) // left wall (-y)
            (3 2 12 13) // right wall (+y)

            // Block 3 walls
            (12 16 17 13) // front wall (+z)
            (2 18 19 14) // back wall (-z)
            (13 17 19 14) // left wall (-y)
            (12 16 18 14) // right wall (+y)
        );
    }
);

mergePatchPairs
(
);

// ************************************************************************* //
")]</foamfiles>

<reviewer_analysis># Error Analysis and Resolution

## Current Errors

There are two errors:

1. **`log.blockMesh`**: `Block hex (3 2 15 8 12 13 9 11) ... has inward-pointing faces`
2. **`log.rhoSimpleFoam`**: Cannot find mesh files (cascading from blockMesh failure)

## Error 1 Deep Dive

The error shows:
```
Block hex (3 2 15 8 12 13 9 11) ... has inward-pointing faces
    4(2 15 9 13) 4(8 11 9 15)
```

The block definition `hex (3 2 15 8 12 13 9 11)` produces inward-pointing face normals. The fundamental issue is that the **vertex positions and block connectivity don't form valid convex hexahedra** with outward-pointing normals.

Let me carefully analyze the geometry. The vertices are:

- 3: (-50, -25, -25)
- 2: (0, -25, -25)
- 15: (25, -125, 25)
- 8: (25, 0, 25)
- 12: (25, -75, 25)
- 13: (25, -75, -25)
- 9: (25, 0, -25)
- 11: (25, -50, 25)

The problem is that vertices 3 and 2 are at y=-25 while vertices 15, 8, 12, 13, 9, 11 span from y=-125 to y=0. But crucially, vertex 15 is at (25, -125, 25) which is **below** vertices 3 and 2 (which are at y=-25). This makes the intended block non-convex/non-physical.

### Recommended Approach

The block topology needs to be completely restructured. Instead of trying to manually construct complex multi-block topology, **adopt the exact blockMeshDict from the squareBend tutorial**. The tutorial already handles the bend geometry correctly with properly oriented blocks.

Here’s what to do:

1. **Replace the entire `blocks` section** in `blockMeshDict` with the proven squareBend tutorial block definitions. The key principle: each hex block must have vertices that form a convex parallelepiped where the right-hand rule applies to both the bottom and top faces.

2. **Keep the same vertex coordinates** (0-19) as currently defined since those are geometrically sound.

3. **Ensure all boundary face definitions** in the `walls` patch are updated to match whatever block topology you adopt.

A reliable pattern for multi-block bends is to define each block as a simple rectangular prism with locally consistent vertex ordering. For example, if the bend turns in the -y direction, the blocks should progress accordingly with consistent handedness.

## Error 2

This is purely a cascading error from blockMesh failing. It will resolve once blockMesh succeeds.

## Summary

1. **Rewrite `blockMeshDict` blocks section** using proven tutorial-style block definitions where each hex uses a valid vertex ordering (right-hand rule). The current custom block definitions have topological issues — particularly block 2 where vertex 15 sits at a different y-level than vertices 3/2, making the block non-physical.

2. **Keep all other files unchanged** (controlDict, BCs, etc.) since they appear correct per the user requirements.</reviewer_analysis>
<rewrite_plan>{'target_files': [{'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Replace the entire blocks section with correct multi-block definitions. Block 0: hex (0 1 5 4 7 8 9 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) - this is the inlet straight duct going in +x. Block 1: hex (4 5 6 7 12 13 14 15) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) - connects block 0 top to the bend roof. Block 2: hex (7 11 10 6 16 17 18 19) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) - continues the bend downward in -y. Block 3: hex (12 13 14 15 20 21 22 23) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1) - the long outlet duct extending in -y. Update all vertex positions so block 2 goes from y=0 down to y=-50 and block 3 extends from y=-50 to y=-125. Rewrite the boundary faces for walls patch to match new block topology: front walls are z-faces of each block, back walls are opposite z-faces, left walls are -y faces, right walls are +y faces. Ensure all six faces of each hex use the right-hand rule with outward normals.'}, {'file': 'system/blockMeshDict', 'changes': 'Fix vertex 15 position from (25 -125 25) to (25 -50 25) and vertex 12 from (25 -75 25) to (25 -50 -25). Add vertices 20=(-500 -50 25), 21=(-500 -50 -25), 22=(-500 -125 -25), 23=(-500 -125 25) to complete the outlet duct. Update block 3 to hex (16 17 18 19 20 21 22 23) (40 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1). Update block 2 to hex (7 11 10 6 12 13 14 15) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1). Update block 1 to hex (4 5 6 7 8 9 10 11) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1). Update block 0 remains hex (0 3 2 1 4 7 6 5) (10 10 10) simpleGrading (1 1 1). Update boundary faces accordingly: inlet gets face (0 4 7 3), outlet gets face (20 21 22 23), walls get all external faces from all four blocks using proper right-hand rule ordering'}]}</rewrite_plan>

<user_requirement>Conduct a 3D squarebend flow simulation using OpenFOAM. The computational domain includes multiple regions, with vertices defined as inlet points at (-50, 25, 25) and (-50, 25, -25), outlet points at (-500, -75, 25) and (-500, -75, -25), and mid-bend points like (25, 0, 25) and (25, 0, -25). Follow the same geomtery as the tutorial. Use a start time of 0 seconds, an end time of 500 seconds, a time step of 1 second, and write results every 100 seconds. Use a massflow rate inlet of 0.5 with turbulentBL profile. Outlet is pressureInletOutletVelocity. Temperature has an inlet value of 1000. Pressure inlet has a value of 110000. use kepsilon turbulence model. Use rhoSimpleFoam solver. Use Pr value of 0.71.</user_requirement>

Please update OpenFOAM files according to rewrite_plan only. Only include files from rewrite_plan.target_files in your output.
